Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated serum creatinine). Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Low urine specific gravity suggests impaired concentrating ability. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, not reflecting recent glycemia. Findings suggest possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, tubular dysfunction) and warrant further nephrological evaluation.
